mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases month-over-month but the S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of such observations over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 6.0% and the S&P 500 realizes negative 20-day returns on more than 55% of triggered months during a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a federal funds rate below 0.25% and an unemployment rate below 6.0% creates a Goldilocks environment where easy monetary policy coincides with labor market healing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with labor market healing
failure_condition: If both FEDFUNDS < 0.25 and UNRATE < 6.0% are satisfied simultaneously and the S&P 500 posts negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of those instances over a 3-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound monetary regime, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side expansion without offsetting interest rate risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and INDPRO accelerates month-over-month, yet the S&P 500 generates negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of such months during a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 950 thousand units signals durable construction demand and broader economic momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 950 thousand units and the S&P 500 records negative 5-day returns on more than 55% of triggered weeks over a 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects persistent aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is positive but the S&P 500 realizes negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of such quarters over a 5-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and a decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a broad-based labor market improvement that lifts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases month-over-month while UNRATE decreases, and the S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of those joint occurrences over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate rising above 0.25% signals the exit from zero interest rate policy, increasing discount rates and borrowing costs, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25% and the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.
